profile.name: Linda
profile.role_in_household: Primary client; manages day-to-day bills and household routines, focuses on keeping spending predictable and avoiding surprises.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate — understands budgeting and basic retirement-account concepts, but prefers plain-English explanations and may need statements to recall provider/platform details.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate but safety-leaning; okay with some market movement if near-term cash needs are clearly covered. Gets uneasy when money feels illiquid or when withdrawals seem improvised.
profile.communication_style: Warm, practical, and a bit hedged: uses 'I think'/'maybe,' speaks in round numbers, and may mix terms like balance vs payment. Asks clarifying questions and repeats back key takeaways to confirm.
emotional_tendencies: calmer with checklists and written summaries
profile.decision_making_style: Consensus-seeking; wants William’s input, then prefers a short list of options and time to think before committing.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously trusting; responds well to transparency on taxes/fees and clear next steps in writing.
hidden_concerns: worries about healthcare or long-term care disrupting plans
concerned about tax mistakes and retirement-account rules
uneasy about private/alternative investments being hard to access

profile.name: William
profile.role_in_household: Spouse/partner; leads on investments and property/income items tied to him, tends to steer discussions on taxes, returns, and tradeoffs.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate-to-high — comfortable with taxable vs retirement distinctions and tax impacts; often remembers specific figures and will correct small inaccuracies, though he can occasionally conflate gross vs net or provider vs account type.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate with a return-seeking tilt; willing to tolerate volatility if the rationale is tax-aware and disciplined. Pushes back if recommendations feel overly conservative or 'cash-heavy.'
profile.communication_style: Direct and numbers-forward; may interrupt to уточнить a figure, uses 'No, it was closer to…' and 'Let me think…'. Skeptical of vague answers and asks pointed follow-ups.
profile.decision_making_style: Analytical and challenge-oriented; wants to see assumptions, tax implications, and liquidity impacts before agreeing, then decides decisively.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Measured; trust builds through demonstrated competence, consistency, and accurate recall of their full picture across income, assets, property, and insurance.
hidden_concerns: wants to preserve control and flexibility as they age
quiet worry about rental/business income variability even if it has been steady
